title: Crataegus × dissecta
intro: Crataegus × dissecta, also known as the hybrid hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote cardiovascular health, with potential benefits including lowering blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Crataegus × dissecta include flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include standardized extracts, capsules, and teas, which are often used to support heart health and alleviate symptoms of hypertension and arrhythmia.
taxonomy: Crataegus × dissecta is a hybrid species within the genus Crataegus, commonly known as hawthorn. It is a member of the Rosaceae family, which includes various other fruit-bearing trees and shrubs. As a hybrid, Crataegus × dissecta is not assigned a specific classification rank like species or subspecies, but rather is recognized for its unique characteristics. The exact classification of Crataegus × dissecta may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a distinct hybrid within the hawthorn genus. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of this specific hybrid species.

benefits: Crataegus × dissecta, a hybrid hawthorn species, is known to have potential health benefits due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's berries and leaves are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which may help protect against cardiovascular diseases and oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that Crataegus × dissecta extracts may also have a positive effect on blood pressure and lipid profiles. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's health benefits and potential applications. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
properties: Crataegus × dissecta is known to possess various therapeutic properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids in its extracts. These compounds have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The extracts of Crataegus × dissecta have also been studied for their potential in treating cardiovascular diseases, including hypertension and atherosclerosis, due to their vasodilatory and antioxidant effects. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been found to have immunomodulatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential in treating various inflammatory and oxidative stress-related diseases. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Crataegus × dissecta.
constituents: Crataegus × dissecta contains flavonoids and other phenolic compounds, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These active constituents are responsible for the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
